meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to overcome deployment hurdles. A beginner-friendly case study using Antigravity to launch your first real-world SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure usually stems from misconfigured environment variables or missing build dependencies. By leveraging Claude Code to audit your repository and using Antigravity to automate your CI/CD pipeline, you can isolate these errors, generate production-ready configurations, and push your application to the cloud with confidence in minutes.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks hunched over your IDE, crafting logic that works perfectly on your local machine. But the moment you reach for that 'Deploy' button, the screen floods with cryptic red text—a wall of errors that feels like a final boss you aren't prepared to fight. This is the 'Deployment Gap,' the silent killer of student passion projects.

But what if you could bypass the frustration of infrastructure engineering? By integrating Claude Code into your workflow, you transform your terminal into a senior DevOps partner. This guide provides the blueprint to bridge the gap between your local prototype and a live, production-grade application, ensuring your portfolio project finally sees the light of day.

steps.title: Initialize Environment Audit
steps.description: Before deploying, we must ensure the local environment matches production requirements. We use Claude to scan for missing .env keys or incompatible versions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project directory for missing production-ready configuration files and generate a checklist for deployment readiness.

steps.title: Configure Build Artifacts
steps.description: Automate the creation of build scripts. This ensures your project compiles correctly in a clean-room environment like Vercel or Railway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized build script for my specific framework that handles dependency installation and minification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate build script for production'

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipeline Setup
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a GitHub Actions workflow so your app deploys automatically whenever you push code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that triggers on push to the main branch and deploys to my cloud provider.

steps.title: Debug Deployment Logs
steps.description: When a deployment fails, use Claude Code to parse the logs instantly rather than scouring StackOverflow manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the following deployment error log in simple terms and provide the exact fix for my configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude chat 'analyze this deployment error: [paste log here]'

steps.title: Final Health Check
steps.description: Perform a final smoke test to ensure the production URL is responsive and the database connection is stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a curl-based health check script that verifies my production endpoint returns a 200 OK status.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to track necessary environment variables; Claude Code can auto-generate this from your code usage.
Ignore node_modules and dist folders in your deployment pipeline to keep build times under 60 seconds.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints before attempting a full deployment to ensure logic integrity.
When facing '403 Forbidden' errors, ask Claude to compare your local CORS settings against your production environment variables.
Treat your deployment script as code; version it in your repository so you can roll back instantly if a production push breaks.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, focus on setting up monitoring tools like Sentry or LogRocket to observe how real users interact with your code, then iterate based on their feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment errors hold back your potential. With these tools, you are no longer just a student—you are a builder with a professional workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
